articles.title: U.S. stages largest-ever annual military drills in the Philippines
articles.summary: U.S. and allies hold their largest drills yet in the Philippines near Taiwan, showcasing new tactics and alliances to deter China—while Beijing stages its own exercises nearby.

articles.title: President Trump’s Sudden U-Turn, and a $1 Billion Ballroom Proposal
articles.summary: Plus, a visit to the “Gates to Hell.”

articles.title: A new Medicare option for weight loss drugs is coming: Here's what to know
articles.summary: Millions of people with Medicare will soon be eligible to get discounted GLP-1 drugs for weight loss. Here's how it will work.

articles.title: World Cup Dynamic Ticket Pricing Drives Argentine Soccer Fans To Extremes
articles.summary: Dynamic ticket pricing has made this year’s tournament the most expensive in history, pushing Argentine fans to extremes.

articles.title: Tofersen, a New Treatment for A.L.S., Reverses Symptoms for Some
articles.summary: The drug is for a small subset of patients. But evidence that breathing and strength can get better for some of them is remarkable for a paralyzing, usually fatal disease.

articles.title: Poll: Trump blamed for gas prices as Democrats gain midterm edge
articles.summary: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds strong support for Democrats this midterm season. It also shows increasing concern about the war in Iran and the economy, particularly the price of gas.

articles.title: A Look Inside the Case That Enshrined Political Power for Billionaires
articles.summary: After Watergate, Congress tried to curtail the role of money in politics. But a pivotal Supreme Court case nipped it in the bud. Years later, new details are emerging on how wealthy Americans were con

articles.title: Skeletons in their clothing: Recovering bodies from the rubble in Gaza
articles.summary: It was one of the deadliest Israeli strikes of the Gaza war, devastating one extended family. A year and a half later, the survivors find their loved ones' remains.

articles.title: Lindsey Vonn's Olympic crash was a horrific setback. But she's never been one to hide
articles.summary: Vonn defied her doubters to race in the Olympics on a torn ACL, but her comeback dream ended with a broken left leg. Most people would want to hide after such a setback — but Vonn isn't most people.

articles.title: A trip to Europe? In this economy? Expensive flights keep vacations closer to home
articles.summary: Travel advisers are seeing Americans picking cheaper domestic travel destinations over Europe.

articles.title: Do you say 'wash' or 'warsh?' Here's where the pronunciation comes from
articles.summary: Trump's nominee for the Federal Reserve chair, Kevin Warsh, has dominated headlines for weeks. For some, his surname is a reminder of a disappearing accent in South Midland America.

articles.title: Trump and Rubio Insist Iran War Is Over, Even as Missiles Fly During Cease-Fire
articles.summary: The White House is turning to rhetorical leaps as President Trump tries to put the biggest political crisis of his presidency behind him.

articles.title: Zelenskyy slams Russia as strikes kill 22 in Ukraine before announced ceasefire
articles.summary: Zelenskyy rebuked Moscow for what he said was its "utter cynicism" in launching the attacks after Russia announced a unilateral ceasefire over two days later this week.

articles.title: Police say Australian women with alleged IS ties face charges on return from Syria
articles.summary: The Australian government had been alerted Wednesday that four women and nine children had booked flights from Damascus to Australia, Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke said.

articles.title: Takeaways From Indiana Primary Elections 2026: Trump Gets Payback
articles.summary: The president helped unseat most of the state lawmakers he targeted after they rebuffed his call to draw new House maps to help Republicans.

articles.title: In Indiana Primary Elections, Most Trump-Backed Challengers Beat Incumbents
articles.summary: President Trump endorsed primary opponents against seven Republican lawmakers who opposed his redistricting push. At least five of the Trump-backed candidates have won their races.

articles.title: What Happened When Pope Leo XIV Had to Call Customer Service
articles.summary: Spoiler alert: There was no miracle.

articles.title: U.S. says it's made Hormuz safe for commercial shipping, but standoff hasn't changed
articles.summary: The administration has announced a new military strategy designed to open up the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping, but it's unclear if this will break Iran's grip on the key waterway.

articles.title: NPR went looking for Polymarket's Panama headquarters. It's elusive
articles.summary: The hugely popular prediction market was shut down by U.S. regulators in 2022 and re-opened in Panama, where it has benefited from tax and legal benefits for years.

articles.title: The Iran war sent jet fuel prices sky-high. Here's what air travelers should know
articles.summary: Fuel is airlines' second-largest expense, and getting even costlier due to the U.S. war with Iran. Airlines are looking for ways to cope, starting by hiking checked baggage fees for most fliers.

articles.title: SCOTUS temporarily restores mifepristone access. And, Indiana, Ohio vote in primaries
articles.summary: The Supreme Court temporarily restored access to the abortion pill mifepristone. And, Indiana's primaries will test President Trump's influence as he tries to oust incumbent Republican senators.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
